document.title: Japan Test Report
authorityOrJurisdiction: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications

sourceSummary.oneLine: Japan supplementary RF test report JR740701-11AC for Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C series 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory conformity evidence for specified radio equipment testing under Japanese technical regulation methods and ARIB STD-T66 / RCR STD-33.
keyPoints: Report No. JR740701-11AC, Rev. 01, was initially issued on Feb. 25, 2021.
The report is supplementary to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AC.
ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final testing.

Summary test results show Pass for antenna power, frequency tolerance, transmitter spurious emission, occupied bandwidth, spreading bandwidth, spreading factor, receiver emission, and interference prevention function.
Carrier sense is N/A for STD-T66 and Pass for STD-33 in the summary of test results.

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

entities.name: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
entities.evidence: the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “43” and “44” of Article 88, Paragraph 1

concepts.name: Supplementary report
concepts.evidence: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AC.

concepts.name: Representative model testing
concepts.evidence: model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

concepts.name: Carrier sense measurement
concepts.evidence: EUT shall not transmit any waves when carrier wave inject into EUT

concepts.name: Interference prevention function
concepts.evidence: The identification code shall be 48 bits long

evidence.claim: The document is Japan Test Report JR740701-11AC, Rev. 01.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: JR740701-11AC Page No. : 1 of 22 Report Version: Rev. 01 Japan Test Report

evidence.claim: The initial issue date is Feb. 25, 2021.
The tested equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The model number identified on the cover is ST60-2230C.
The applicant is Laird Connectivity, Inc.
The applied regulatory articles are Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19 and Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19-2.
evidence.evidenceText: Standard : Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19 Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19-2
evidence.pageTarget: Japan Radio Law Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19

evidence.claim: The measurement method references Japanese technical regulations certification methods, MIC notification Annex 43 and 44, and ARIB STD-T66 / RCR STD-33.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement was conducted by the following test method: the test method of Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification etc. of Specified Radio Equipment in Annex 1, the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “43” and “44” of Article 88, Paragraph 1 and ARIB STD-T66 / RCR STD-33

evidence.claim: The report is supplementary to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AC.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AC.

evidence.claim: The supplementary changes include additional model and product names, applicant and brand changes, CONFIG_HOST0/1/2 resistance differences, and latest software version update.
evidence.evidenceText: The modification is 1. Additional model and product names, changing applicant and brand name. 2. Resistance of CONFIG_HOST0/1/2 are different to switch interface 3. Update to the latest SW version

evidence.claim: The report states the changes do not impact the original test results.
evidence.evidenceText: After verification, above changes does not impact to original test results. Thus test results remain the same.

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final testing.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The EUT frequency range is 2412 to 2472 MHz and 2484 MHz.
The operating modes include 802.11b, 802.11g, 802.11n HT20, and 802.11n HT40.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating Mode: IEEE Std. 802.11 / Data rate (Mbps/MCS) 802.11b: Up to 11 Mbps 802.11g: Up to 54 Mbps 802.11n HT 20 (MCS 0~15) 802.11n HT 40 (MCS 0~15)

evidence.claim: The summary reports Pass results for key STD-T66 tests except Carrier Sense, which is N/A.
evidence.evidenceText: Ref. Std. Clause (STD-T66) Description Result ... Antenna Power Pass ... Interference prevention function Pass ... Carrier Sense N/A*

evidence.claim: The summary reports Pass results for key STD-33 tests including Carrier Sense.
evidence.evidenceText: Ref. Std. Clause (STD-33) Description Result ... Antenna Power Pass ... Interference prevention function Pass ... Carrier Sense Pass

evidence.claim: No deviation from the test standard and measurement procedure is reported.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.4 Deviation from Test Standard and Measurement Procedure None

evidence.claim: The test laboratory was International Certification Corp. at TH01-WS in Kwei Shan, Taiwan.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Laboratory International Certification Corp. Test Site TH01-WS Address of Test Site (Kwei Shan) No. 3-1, Lane 6, Wen San 3rd St., Kwei Shan District, Tao Yuan City 333, Taiwan, R.O.C.

evidence.claim: Carrier sensing function appendix summary shows Pass for 802.11n HT40 with -42.25 dBm and Good function.
Interference prevention function appendix summary shows Pass with ID C0:EE:40:40:02:7C, 48 bits, Good.

reviewFlags.reason: Carrier Sense Measurement section 3.6.4 says 'Refer to Appendix N', but the table of contents and provided appendix identify Carrier Sense Measurement as Appendix F.

authoritySignal metadata is 'general'; jurisdiction and authority were inferred only from the report title, Japanese regulatory references, and Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification text.
